definition: Ground-level ozone (O₃) is a reactive gas formed when pollutants from vehicles and industry react in sunlight. Ozone can irritate the airways and is commonly measured by regulatory monitors. GENARCH summarizes population-level evidence linking ozone exposure to respiratory outcomes without making individual medical claims.
name: Ground-Level Ozone

references.title: Outdoor air pollution and asthma in children

sensitive_windows.mechanism_rationale: Outdoor activity patterns and episodic high-ozone events can influence exposure dose; cohort studies often evaluate school-age and adolescent outcomes.

systems_affected.mechanism_summary: Ozone is an oxidant that can trigger airway irritation and inflammatory signaling; effects vary by exposure pattern and baseline health in cohort studies.
